commands.review_summary:
The PR changes diagnostics-otel log export so withheld log message bodies use `[message redacted]`, adds an internal `openclaw.log.body_redacted` marker, blocks caller spoofing of that marker, and updates OTEL docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +16, Docs +2. Total +28 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main's diagnostic log builder emits `body: "log"` whenever log body capture is disabled, and existing focused tests assert that placeholder. I did not run the test suite because this review is read-only and the PR body already notes the focused Vitest proof was not completed.
commands.review_followup: - This maintainer/draft PR should stay in human review until the exported log body compatibility change is accepted and focused diagnostics-otel validation is available.

target.title: fix: make redacted OTEL log bodies explicit
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators exporting OpenClaw diagnostic logs to OTEL or stdout JSONL would see the placeholder body `log` when log message capture was disabled. That made an intentionally withheld message look like the actual log text was `log`.

## Why This Change Was Made

Withheld diagnostic log bodies now export as `[message redacted]` and include the reserved `openclaw.log.body_redacted: true` attribute. The marker is internal-only, so caller-supplied diagnostic attributes cannot spoof or overwrite it, and broad `diagnostics.otel.captureContent: true` still exports the normalized/redacted real message body without the marker.

## User Impact

Operators and downstream log processors can reliably distinguish redacted diagnostic log bodies from real message content without weakening the existing content-capture privacy boundary.

## Evidence

Behavioral proof:

- Focused diagnostics-otel tests now cover stdout-only export, stdout+OTLP export, default OTLP export, granular `captureContent.*`, broad `captureContent: true`, trusted trace-context logs, caller attempts to spoof `openclaw.log.body_redacted`, and max-attribute pressure around the reserved marker.
- Documentation now states that withheld OTEL log bodies use `[message redacted]` and `openclaw.log.body_redacted: true`.

Verification:

- `git diff --check` passed.
- `node scripts/docs-list.js docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md` passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed with no accepted/actionable findings after fixing its marker-overwrite and marker-cap findings.
- Not run locally: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; this worktree cannot hydrate `node_modules` because `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` repeatedly fails fetching `@openclaw/crabline@0.1.5` from the configured Atlassian npm proxy with HTTP 404.
- Not completed remotely before PR creation: delegated Blacksmith Testbox proof stayed queued for about 11 minutes and was canceled before the final patch was committed.
target.author: jesse-merhi
